rationale: A model is an L-structure (def:axiom-systems-structure) of a theory (def:axiom-systems-theory) in which every sentence of T is true. That 'true in M' is precisely the satisfaction relation, which was missing: def:axiom-systems-satisfaction is added as the load-bearing notion doing the actual semantic work.
licensing_quote:
if every sentence in $T$ is true in
$\mathfrak{M}$
notes: Structure and theory locate the objects; satisfaction is what makes 'model of T' a substantive condition rather than mere typing.
